persona.name: The High-Volume All-Category Power Spender
persona.tagline: Shops everywhere, often, and has been doing it for over a year and a half.
persona.description: These 182 customers spend across every category at 40–120% above average, with grocery POS alone hitting $20,554/yr and shopping POS at $15,922/yr. They run nearly 1,900 transactions a year — that's over 5 per day — and have stayed loyal for 17–18 months straight across most categories. The only relative weak spot is gas and transport, which sits 40% below average.
traits: Spends 2.2x the average on online groceries — the single biggest above-average signal in this cluster
Averages 134 shopping POS and 132 grocery POS transactions per year — near-daily in-store activity
Loyalty runs 17–18 consecutive months across almost every category
Entertainment spend of $9,134/yr at $90/transaction suggests regular outings or subscriptions
Gas and transport is the one laggard at 0.6x average, suggesting these customers may live close to shops or use delivery

persona.name: The Rare Big-Ticket Online Shopper
persona.tagline: Barely transacts, but when they do, they spend hundreds at a time online.
persona.description: These 11 customers make only about 11 transactions a year across all categories, but their average transaction is $587 — driven by online shopping at $1,017/txn and misc online at $794/txn. Consecutive months of engagement are just 1.0 or less, meaning they show up in bursts rather than habitually. They are nearly invisible in daily spending categories like food, home, and kids.
traits: Average transaction of $587 — 6x higher than typical — driven by rare but large online purchases
Online shopping averages $1,017 per transaction with only 3.2 transactions per year
76.5% of transactions are classified as high-value, the second highest rate across all clusters
Consecutive engagement is just 1 month or less — these are one-off or very infrequent buyers
Almost no spend on food, dining, home, kids, or gas — this is not a lifestyle spender

persona.name: The Light, Sporadic Across-the-Board Undershopper
persona.tagline: Active in every category, but at roughly a third to a half of normal levels.
persona.description: These 227 customers spend about 37–42% of the typical rate across every single category — no standout high or low, just uniformly low volume. They average 530 transactions a year (versus 1,296–1,869 for active clusters) and their consecutive months of loyalty trail off in entertainment, food, and health to just 9–10 months. They look like occasional rather than habitual users of the bank's card.
traits: Every category sits at 0.37–0.42x average — unusually uniform underperformance with no category standing out
Only 530 transactions per year, roughly 40% of what the average active cluster shows
Grocery POS is their biggest spend at $4,448/yr but still well below average at 0.41x
Consecutive months drop to 9–10 for entertainment, food, and health — these are lapsing or seasonal categories
Travel is their most inconsistent habit at just 5.2 consecutive months and 0.42x average spend

persona.name: The High-Frequency Ghost — Active Spend Pattern, Zero Loyalty Signal
persona.tagline: Looks like a big spender in volume, but consecutive months are nearly zero — something doesn't add up.
persona.description: These 18 customers show transaction counts and spend levels that look almost normal (0.85–0.96x average across categories), but their consecutive loyalty months are 0.1–0.9 — essentially zero. Combined with an average transaction of $592 and 79.7% high-value transactions, this cluster behaves like Cluster 1 and 5 in structure but has a much fuller category footprint. This is the most anomalous cluster in the dataset and may reflect data quality issues, newly opened accounts with large opening transactions, or customers with consolidated batch-style statements.
traits: Transaction volumes per category look near-normal (0.85–0.96x) but consecutive months are 0.1–0.9 — near zero
Average transaction of $592 is about 8x higher than Cluster 6/7/8 siblings, despite similar category mix
79.7% of transactions are high-value — inconsistent with the low average per-transaction in categories like food ($52) and personal care ($47)
Gas and transport shows 76 transactions/yr at $5,354 — but only 0.9 consecutive months of activity
This cluster may represent accounts with irregular data, batch imports, or onboarding-period spending

persona.name: The Ultra-Rare Luxury Online Shopper
persona.tagline: Almost never uses their card, but when they do, it's for big online purchases.
persona.description: These 11 customers make fewer than 11 transactions a year, but their average transaction is $655 — the highest of any cluster. Online shopping alone averages $989/transaction and in-store shopping $937/transaction. They have 89% high-value transactions and near-zero consecutive loyalty months, making them the most infrequent but highest per-transaction spenders in the dataset.
traits: Highest average transaction value at $655 — driven by shopping_net at $989/txn and shopping_pos at $937/txn
89% of transactions are classified as high-value — the highest rate across all clusters
Only about 11 total transactions per year across all categories combined
Consecutive months are 0–1.1 — essentially no repeat habit in any category
Almost no spend on food, dining, gas, travel, or entertainment — this is a pure big-ticket online buyer

persona.name: The Committed Commuter & Steady Household Manager
persona.tagline: Unlike siblings 7 and 8, this group's standout is gas spending near average — not above it — with a calmer, more balanced profile across all categories.
persona.description: Cluster 6 is the quietest of the three sub-clusters. Gas and transport is their top category at 1.14x average — noticeable, but far below siblings 7 (1.69x) and 8 (1.79x). Where siblings 7 and 8 push many categories above average, Cluster 6 sits right around or just below average in most areas, with grocery POS at 0.95x and shopping at 0.84–0.86x. They are loyal — 17–18 months in most categories — but spend at a more restrained level than either sibling.
traits: Gas is their top signal at 1.14x average but is significantly lower than siblings at 1.69x and 1.79x
Most categories cluster tightly between 0.81x and 1.04x — the most balanced, least extreme of the three sub-clusters
Grocery POS spend of $9,912/yr and 89.7 transactions suggest regular but not heavy in-store grocery shopping
Loyalty is strong — 17–18 consecutive months across most categories — matching siblings in commitment
Online grocery (grocery_net) is a weak spot at 0.44x average, unlike sibling 8 which is strong there at 1.69x

persona.name: The High-Mileage Commuter Who Also Travels and Stays Active
persona.tagline: Unlike siblings 6 and 8, this group pairs heavy gas use with above-average travel spend and the highest travel cost-per-trip.
persona.description: Cluster 7 is defined by gas at 1.69x average and travel at 1.45x average with $147/transaction — far above sibling 6's $40/txn and sibling 8's $43/txn. This suggests real travel (flights, hotels) rather than local trips. They also lead siblings in health/fitness (1.35x), home (1.34x), and entertainment (1.32x), painting a picture of an active, mobile lifestyle with genuine travel expenditure — not just commuting.
traits: Travel averages $147/transaction — 3.5x higher than sibling 6 ($40) and 3.4x higher than sibling 8 ($43), suggesting flights or hotels
Gas at 1.69x average is high but still below sibling 8's 1.79x — commuting is heavy but travel is the true differentiator
Health and fitness at 1.35x and entertainment at 1.32x suggest an active social and physical lifestyle
Kids and pets at 1.36x and home at 1.34x point to a busy household managing both family and travel
Grocery_net at 0.94x is far stronger than sibling 6's 0.44x, suggesting some online grocery adoption alongside in-store habits

persona.name: The Highest-Frequency Everyday Spender with Strong Online Grocery Habit
persona.tagline: Unlike siblings 6 and 7, this group leads in sheer transaction volume and is the only one with a notably above-average online grocery habit.
persona.description: Cluster 8 runs 2,036 transactions per year — more than any other sub-cluster or top-level cluster except Cluster 0. Gas tops the charts at 1.79x average, and unlike siblings, grocery_net is 1.69x average (58.5 transactions/yr at $2,863), suggesting a real online grocery subscription or repeat ordering habit. Food and dining at 1.47x and misc_pos at 1.56x show this group is spending constantly across everyday categories at a pace that feels almost automated.
traits: Highest transaction count of all sub-clusters at 2,036/yr — about 57% more than sibling 6's 1,297/yr
Online grocery (grocery_net) at 1.69x average with 58.5 transactions/yr — the only sub-cluster where this is a meaningful signal
Gas at 1.79x is the highest of the three siblings, suggesting the most vehicle-dependent lifestyle
Food and dining at 1.47x average and $38.53/transaction suggests frequent fast-casual or quick service meals
Travel average of $43/transaction is the lowest of the three siblings, confirming this group drives more than it flies
